title: Finding the total cost of a terms arrangement
problem: A sofa has a cash price of $\$1400$. It can be purchased on terms with a deposit of $\$200$ and 18 monthly repayments of $\$75$. Find the total amount paid under the terms arrangement.

steps.instruction: Identify the deposit and the repayment details.
steps.working: Deposit $= \$200$, number of repayments $= 18$, each repayment $= \$75$

steps.instruction: Calculate the total of all monthly repayments.
Add the deposit to find the total amount paid.

title: Expressing the interest cost as a percentage of the cash price
problem: A dishwasher has a cash price of $\$1200$. Buying on terms costs a deposit of $\$240$ and 12 monthly repayments of $\$90$. Find the interest paid and express it as a percentage of the cash price.

steps.instruction: Find the total amount paid on terms.
Subtract the cash price to find the interest charged.
Express the interest as a percentage of the cash price.

answer: The interest paid is $\$120$, which is $10\%$ of the cash price.

title: Comparing two credit options to find the cheaper deal
problem: A gaming console has a cash price of $\$600$. Option A is a buy-now-pay-later arrangement with a flat fee of $\$30$ and the full balance split into 4 equal fortnightly payments. Option B is buying on terms with a deposit of $\$100$ and 10 monthly repayments of $\$58$. Find the total cost of each option and determine which is cheaper and by how much.

steps.instruction: Find the total cost of Option A (BNPL). The balance itself is still paid in full — the flat fee is the only extra cost.
Find the total cost of Option B (terms).
Compare the two totals to find which is cheaper and by how much.

answer: Option A (BNPL) costs $\$630$ and Option B (terms) costs $\$680$. Option A is cheaper by $\$50$.
